raw_consumer_statement:
Costco Citi Fraud Response scam. 

On Friday XX/XX/year>. 
I received a call from XXXX and XXXX. I answered the XXXX at XXXX. 
Someone with a foreign accent claimed they were with Costco Citi Card Fraud department, asking if I was purchasing something in Texas, XXXX. I said no, and they stated a few other charges which had supposedly been made. They requested me to verify my personal information. It seemed weird, but I submitted and gave my personal information to confirm my account. Because I confirmed my information, they changed my login and email address. They then sent an email confirming another email address, which was changed to : XXXX. They explained that the purchasers had done that, but it was really the people on the phone. 
During the conversation I looked at my mobile application and did not see any pending charges. I was wondering why I didnt see anything. On a second attempt to login, the program would not let me login. 
At the end of the conversation, he said I needed to hang up and then dial a number so that Citi text alert messages would be routed back to me. But it was actually the other way around, and they call forwarded my number. This is the number they had me input. XXXX # My husband was trying to contact me on my personal line and a man answered the phone. The man eventually stopped answering. My husband switched and contacted me on my business number so he was able to tell me what was occurring. 
That is when I realized the fraud and I called the XXXX Citi card number on the back of my card and they stated they had not called me and no purchases had been made. They cancelled my card and fix my account information. I then called XXXX to cancel the call forwarding. I also went to a store to check for any virus on the phone, which was not apparent the technician. 
Earlier in the week I also received phone calls claiming that I had to buy QB support for my subscription. But I already fell for that one a few years back and did not purchase.
